title: Marathon Petroleum Corporation (MPC) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Marathon Petroleum Corporation (MPC) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% growth rate is estimated based on the company's historical free cash flow generation and near-term market expectations.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate reflects the estimated weighted average cost of capital, accounting for the risk-free rate and company-specific risk premiums.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate is used, aligning with long-term macroeconomic GDP growth projections.

thesisTitle: The Investment Thesis for Marathon Petroleum Corporation
thesisParagraphs: The current market price of 235.78 reflects specific expectations about Marathon Petroleum Corporation's future growth and cash flow generation capabilities. Our DCF model evaluates the fundamental value of the business based on its ability to generate sustainable free cash flows over the next decade.

With an estimated intrinsic value of 134.14, the stock currently appears overvalued. Investors should weigh this valuation against the company's competitive moat, capital allocation strategy, and macroeconomic sensitivities before making investment decisions.
verdictFooter: Based on recent actuals. Data via Yahoo Finance (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did the model pick a 3.0% growth rate for MPC?
faq.answer: The model uses a 3.0% growth rate based on historical free cash flow growth and industry projections over the medium term.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for MPC's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.0% discount rate (WACC) was selected to properly discount future cash flows back to present value, given the risk profile of the business.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely solely on this DCF model for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. A DCF model is highly sensitive to its inputs (growth rate, discount rate, terminal rate). It should be used as one tool among many in a comprehensive investment analysis.
